frame.mexico_role: ACS composition weights only — no scalar Mexican lifetime NPV
frame.education_bucket_weights: NAS education cells weighted by ACS Mexico mix (~47% <HS among FB 25–64); Hanson w23753 supplies time-varying <HS composition, not a single ACS snapshot
frame.descendant_band: Optimistic bound: Abramitzky-Boustan w26408 intergenerational mobility; Pessimistic bound: Duncan-Trejo w24394 Hispanic 3rd+ stagnation + ethnic-attrition measurement bias
frame.unnamed_assumption_flagged: Fiscal ledger = native taxpayer wallet only (excludes remittances, origin-country human-capital subsidy, political-economy preference shifts)

parameter_claims.notes: Undocumented accounted for ~two-thirds of foreign-born adults with ≤12 years schooling as of 2013.
Low-skilled foreign-born working-age stock stable post-recession despite undocumented decline — attrition channel active.
More than doubled 1990–2007; composition shifted toward Mexico/Latin America.

parameter_claims.population: foreign-born with high school education or less
parameter_claims.notes: Mexico share peaked 2005 at 48.1% (≤HS) / 64.0% (<HS); paper treats ACS/Census skill bins as neutral cross-national comparator — maps directly to NAS <HS cell weights.

parameter_claims.population: second-generation immigrants vs US-born children at parental 25th percentile
parameter_claims.notes: Historical and modern cohorts; at 75th percentile advantage only 2–5 (past) and <2 (today). Supports optimistic descendant earnings-path offset to NAS.
Controlling for US region reduces immigrant mobility advantage 70%; same-county comparison eliminates gap — unnamed assumption that location choice is separable from immigrant fiscal externality.
Deficit falls from >50% (1st gen) to 21% (2nd gen) but no further decline at 3rd+ — pessimistic descendant band for Mexico-heavy cells.

parameter_claims.population: third-generation Mexican youth ages 16–17 high-school dropout rate
parameter_claims.notes: Dropout rate 25% higher when sample limited to youth who still identify as Mexican; selective ethnic attrition may understate true 3rd+ progress — key falsifier for Abramitzky optimistic band.
7.5% increase; closed citizen–noncitizen graduation gap by 40%; ~49,000 additional Hispanic HS diplomas attributed to DACA.

parameter_claims.population: single US immigrant by education/age cell
parameter_claims.notes: Average immigrant +$7.4k lifetime; survey flags that generational-accounting studies typically assume no return migration — systematic upward bias in assimilation and fiscal duration.
Recent (post-2000) EEA +£22.1bn; recent EEA+non-EEA +£25bn; non-EEA negative overall. Second-gen descendants counted as immigrants until age 16 only — descendant NPV line omitted for adults.

parameter_claims.value_text: immigrants with less than high school typically net fiscal burden
parameter_claims.population: US immigrants <HS (NRC 1997 cited in model motivation)
parameter_claims.notes: Political-economy model: proportional labor tax finances demogrant; unskilled voters' preferred policies prevail most often regardless of group size — fiscal=taxpayer redistribution, not efficiency.

parameter_claims.population: US migrant stock by origin country
parameter_claims.notes: Hurricane-induced immigration positive only above ~0.86% prior stock (~70th percentile); at 90th percentile (5.6%) 1-SD hurricane shock → inflow 0.029% of origin population — network lowers fixed migration cost.
No respondent group average misperception below 15pp; priming immigration before redistribution questions reduces support including real charity donations — welfare-free-rider belief dominates facts.
Younger immigrant inflows can alleviate fiscal pressures in aging destinations; remittance outflows are private transfer offset absent from NAS taxpayer-wallet ledger.

generators.name: Static ACS snapshot vs Hanson composition time series
generators.prompt: For each origin×education_bucket cell in the scenario ledger, compare (a) a single ACS 2023 composition weight to (b) a Hanson w23753 time-varying weight series (1990–2015 Mexico/Latin America <HS shares, post-2007 inflow slowdown). Where does a static snapshot mis-state the stock entering the NAS <HS age-25 NPV cell?
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced Hanson 510k→−160k undocumented swing, Mexico share peaking 2005 then declining, and the brainstorm memo's Round 8 'composition over time' angle before building origin_fiscal_scenario with fixed ACS weights.
generators.negative_space: Standing frame treats ACS 2023 as sufficient composition prior; excludes epochal composition drift and attrition-driven stock stabilization.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Cross-sectional ACS weight is interchangeable with cohort arrival-year composition
Mexico ≈ NAS <HS cell without time index
generators.theory_tested: Time-varying Hanson weights vs static ACS weights change Mexico-heavy NAS cell NPV aggregation

generators.name: Return migration / attrition ledger (CHCP blocked)
generators.prompt: Build an attrition sensitivity band on lifetime NPV: (1) Lubotsky/Dustmann selective re-migration bias on assimilation, (2) Hanson post-2007 net undocumented outflow, (3) Duncan-Trejo ethnic attrition on 3rd+ Hispanic measurement. Flag data gaps: Aydemir-Robinson CHCP working paper HTML-only trap; no US fiscal micro for return migration. What ledger line adjusts NPV horizon when attrition is negatively selected?
generators.retrodiction: Would have flagged brainstorm Round 8 item 10 (CHCP blocked) and Dustmann survey §5.9 return-migration gap before treating SIPP annual proxy as full lifetime residence.
generators.negative_space: Lifetime fiscal models assume immigrants remain until death; cross-sectional assimilation overstates economic success.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Immigrant remains in host country full horizon
CPS 3rd+ generation identifies true descendants without ethnic attrition
generators.theory_tested: Attrition rate × selective outflow direction shifts expected years in US for federal transfer and payroll ledger lines

data_gaps.notes: HTML trap per brainstorm Round 8; Canada paywall — no clean US public fiscal attrition micro

generators.name: Descendant NPV as separate ledger line
generators.prompt: Split lifetime ledger into (A) first-generation immigrant NPV from NAS education×age cells and (B) descendant offset line with two bounds: Abramitzky w26408 (+3–6 percentile mobility at bottom; geographic sorting explains 70%) vs Duncan-Trejo w24394 (Hispanic 2nd→3rd+ earnings stall; ethnic attrition may understate progress). UK Dustmann-Frattini shows descendant accounting breaks at age 16. Where is descendant NPV missing from compose_scenario_ledger?
generators.retrodiction: Would have paired brainstorm Round 5 items 3–4 (descendant upside/downside) and UK methodology comparator before collapsing descendants into same NAS cell as parents.
generators.negative_space: NAS descendant column treated as single calibrated offset; no explicit Abramitzky–Duncan-Trejo band or separate ledger row.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Second-generation fiscal externalities net to zero within parent education cell
CPS third+ generation identifies immigrant descendants without selective ethnic attrition
generators.theory_tested: Descendant earnings/education path as additive NPV line item vs embedded in parent NAS cell

generators.name: Political economy of transfers (taxpayer wallet frame)
generators.prompt: Audit lifetime fiscal memos for the unnamed assumption fiscal impact = native taxpayer wallet only. Cross-read Razin-Sadka-Swagel (demogrant coalitions; unskilled policy dominance), Alesina w24733/w25562 (immigration salience → less redistribution; welfare-state size heterogeneity), and IMF remittance offset. What transfers are excluded from the ledger (remittances, origin-country education subsidy, voter preference shift)?
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced brainstorm Round 3 item 4 (Alesina redistribution) and Round 5 political-economy pairing before treating NAS net fiscal as welfare-neutral accounting.
generators.negative_space: Technical NPV treated as sufficient for policy; political feedback on transfer generosity and native fiscal capacity ignored.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Fiscal = taxes paid minus benefits received by immigrant household only
Native redistribution preferences invariant to immigrant composition
Remittances and origin-country human-capital investment are out of scope
generators.theory_tested: Taxpayer-wallet NPV vs political-economy equilibrium transfer rate under varying immigrant skill mix

generators.name: Legal-status → education-bucket transition path
generators.prompt: Map legal-status shocks (DACA w24315: +5.9pp HS completion, 40% citizen gap closure; Hanson undocumented share of <HS stock) to education_bucket transitions in the scenario ledger. Does a legalization path move cells from <HS toward HS, changing NAS NPV sign within the same ACS origin weight?
generators.retrodiction: Would have connected brainstorm Round 4 Kuka DACA item to education-bucket composition before treating legal status as orthogonal to NAS cell assignment.
generators.negative_space: Legal status treated as parallel unauthorized earnings path, not as human-capital investment response shifting education bucket.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Unauthorized share is a scalar on earnings, not a state machine across education buckets
generators.theory_tested: Legalization elasticity on HS completion × NAS education-cell NPV step function

generators.name: Migrant networks and destination-selection composition
generators.prompt: Borjas-Monras w23756: hurricane shocks × prior US migrant stock (threshold 0.86%) → legal immigration via family reunification. Abramitzky w22381: name-based assimilation closes half the gap in 20 years. How does network-driven destination selection reweight state/PUMA fiscal incidence vs national ACS composition weights?
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced network-fixed-cost mechanism and border-state concentration (Hanson table 3) before applying national Mexico weights to state stage5 context.
generators.negative_space: Origin composition weights applied nationally; destination network sorting and border-state hours-share ignored.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Immigrants distribute proportionally to ACS national origin stock
Migration fixed costs are homogeneous across origin countries
generators.theory_tested: Network-threshold origin inflows × state destination sorting vs flat ACS national weights

theories_tested.theory: Static ACS Mexico <HS weight is sufficient for NAS cell aggregation
theories_tested.prediction: Time-varying Hanson weights (Mexico share peak 2005, post-2007 slowdown) do not materially change weighted NPV vs 2023 ACS snapshot for recent-surge slice
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Join acs_origin_national_2023 education mix to hypothetical hanson_composition_weights_by_decade; compare weighted NAS <HS NPV on origin_fiscal_scenario_2023 Mexico rows
theories_tested.falsifier: ≥10% NPV difference between static and 2005-peak composition weights for YOEP≥2014 <HS adults

theories_tested.theory: Abramitzky optimistic descendant band dominates Duncan-Trejo pessimistic band for Mexico-heavy cells
theories_tested.prediction: Descendant offset line is positive (mobility advantage +3–6 percentiles) after geographic controls
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Add descendant_ledger_line with bounds from w26408 (+mobility) and w24394 (3rd+ earnings stall + ethnic attrition); sensitivity on sipp_scenario_ledger education_bucket transitions
theories_tested.falsifier: Duncan-Trejo 3rd+ stagnation + 25% ethnic-attrition dropout bias flips descendant line negative for Mexican CPS-identified 3rd+ gen

theories_tested.theory: Lifetime NPV horizon unbiased without return-migration adjustment
theories_tested.prediction: Hanson post-2007 net outflow and Lubotsky selective re-migration do not change expected years-in-US by >5%
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Apply attrition_scenario table (low/base/high expected residence years) to federal_net_proxy_annual × horizon; flag NULL until CHCP micro acquired
theories_tested.falsifier: Attrition sensitivity moves Mexico <HS cell sign at 3% discount; CHCP remains blocked — manual band only

theories_tested.theory: Fiscal ledger = taxpayer wallet is policy-sufficient
theories_tested.prediction: Alesina immigration-salience reduction in redistribution support is uncorrelated with immigrant fiscal NPV sign in high-immigrant PUMAs
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Cross-walk origin_puma_household_stage5_context to Alesina mechanism (welfare-free-rider belief); no DuckDB table yet — register as FRAMING-SENSITIVE overlay not in warehouse
theories_tested.falsifier: High <HS immigrant-share PUMAs show federal-positive ledger but political-economy generator predicts transfer restriction — taxpayer wallet omits equilibrium policy response

disconfirmation: Abramitzky w26408: immigrant children more upwardly mobile at bottom of distribution — weakens descendant-pessimism scalar
Duncan-Trejo: Hispanic Americans may have progressed more beyond 2nd generation than CPS data show (ethnic attrition) — widens descendant band upward
Dustmann-Frattini UK: EEA recent immigrants net-positive even under 'worst case' immigrant-side allocation — composition (skill×recency) flips sign within same accounting grammar
Dustmann survey: Storesletten −$36k to +$96k range — education bucket dominates; composition matters more than origin label
IMF G20: aging + automation may reduce low-skill immigration rebound — Hanson structural slowdown disconfirms static surge composition
IZA dp18218 (AGI/automation): joint automation+immigration shocks amplify non-college losses — lifetime earnings path may deteriorate even if fiscal transfers stable
extraction_method: pdftotext full-document on 13 PDFs; frame from immigration-lifetime-dataset-brainstorm-2026-06-15.md
